title: Big Technology AI Summit (full): Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, Aaron Levie & Friends of The Podcast
summary: The Big Technology AI Summit held in San Francisco on June 18, 2026, brought together AI leaders, journalists, and policymakers to discuss the federal government's forced recall of Anthropic's frontier models Fable and Mythos. Speakers debated whether the recall represents an effective AI pause, implications for US competitiveness against China, infrastructure buildout trends, enterprise AI budgets, cybersecurity risks, and OpenAI's compute-driven vision for AI's future.
writeup: This full session from the Big Technology AI Summit, held in San Francisco on June 18, 2026, convenes AI leaders, journalists, and policymakers around the federal government's forced recall of Anthropic's frontier models Fable and Mythos. Speakers including Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, and Aaron Levie debate whether the recall functions as an effective AI pause and what it means for US competitiveness against China. The discussion also covers infrastructure buildout trends, enterprise AI budgets, cybersecurity risks, and OpenAI's compute-driven vision for AI's future.
